title_en: Salah: Prayer in the Hanafi Tradition
title_id: Salat: Ibadah Salat dalam Tradisi Hanafi
content_en:
Prayer in the Hanafi tradition as presented in Al-Ikhtiyar reflects the school's comprehensive approach to worship, combining textual evidence with sophisticated legal reasoning. Al-Mawsili begins with the establishment of prayer times, grounding each in the hadith of Jibril (the Angel Gabriel's teaching the Prophet the prayer times) and the hadith of 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr narrated in Abu Dawud and Al-Nasa'i.

The Hanafi school has some of the most carefully specified prayer time rules among the four schools. For 'Asr, the Hanafi school (following Imam Abu Hanifa) holds that the preferred time begins when the shadow of everything is twice its own length plus the meridian shadow — a later starting point than the other three schools. This was Imam Abu Hanifa's position based on his reading of the relevant hadiths. His two major students, Abu Yusuf and Muhammad ibn al-Hasan, agreed with the other schools that 'Asr begins when the shadow equals the object's length plus the meridian shadow. Al-Mawsili notes that the ruling for fatwa follows the students' position.

The farida (obligatory act) of salah in the Hanafi school are enumerated. The school distinguishes between fara'id (absolute obligations whose omission invalidates the prayer), wajibat (obligations whose intentional omission invalidates but whose forgetful omission requires sujud as-sahw), and sunan (recommended acts). The fara'id include: the opening takbir (which must use the specific words 'Allahu Akbar' according to the Hanafi school — a stricter position than other schools), the standing posture, al-Fatihah, adding a surah after al-Fatihah in the first two rak'ahs (a wajib unique to the Hanafi school), ruku', sujud, the sitting position (qi'dah akhirah), and the closing salam.

The Hanafi school's position on reciting al-Fatihah is significant: it is wajib rather than fard. This means that one who forgets al-Fatihah entirely and recites another Quranic passage instead has still prayed validly (though wrongly), and must perform sujud as-sahw. This differs sharply from the Shafi'i and Hanbali schools, which make al-Fatihah a pillar (rukn) whose omission invalidates the prayer regardless.

Al-Mawsili discusses the Hanafi position on the congregational prayer (jama'ah). The Hanafi school considers jama'ah to be wajib for adult males in ordinary circumstances — not merely recommended (as the Maliki and Shafi'i schools hold) but not as strictly obligatory as the Hanbali school's position. Missing congregation without valid excuse incurs sinfulness without invalidating the prayer itself.

The imam's role is treated in detail. The Hanafi school specifies who may lead prayer: a man may lead other men, women, or mixed groups. A woman may not lead men. A child who has reached the age of discernment may lead other children but not adults in obligatory prayers. The imam's prayer must be at least as valid as that of the followers; if the imam's prayer is invalid due to a condition the followers were unaware of, the followers' prayer is still valid according to the Hanafi school — a position of considerable pastoral importance.

The Hanafi school's treatment of the witr prayer is distinctive: it is wajib (obligatory but not fard), performed as three rak'ahs with a qunut supplication in the third rak'ah after ruku'. This differs from the Shafi'i and Maliki schools' one-rak'ah witr and the Hanbali school's variable witr.
content_id:
Salat dalam tradisi Hanafi sebagaimana disajikan dalam Al-Ikhtiyar mencerminkan pendekatan mazhab yang komprehensif terhadap ibadah, memadukan dalil tekstual dengan penalaran hukum yang canggih. Al-Mawsili memulai dengan penetapan waktu-waktu salat, mendasarkan masing-masingnya pada hadis Jibril (malaikat Jibril yang mengajarkan Nabi ﷺ waktu-waktu salat) dan hadis 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr yang diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud dan an-Nasa'i.

Mazhab Hanafi memiliki aturan waktu salat yang paling terperinci di antara empat mazhab. Untuk salat Asar, mazhab Hanafi (mengikuti Imam Abu Hanifah) berpendapat bahwa waktu yang diutamakan dimulai ketika bayangan segala sesuatu mencapai dua kali panjangnya ditambah bayangan saat matahari di puncak — titik awal yang lebih lambat dibandingkan ketiga mazhab lainnya. Inilah posisi Imam Abu Hanifah berdasarkan pembacaannya atas hadis-hadis yang relevan. Kedua murid utamanya, Abu Yusuf dan Muhammad ibn al-Hasan, sepakat dengan mazhab-mazhab lain bahwa Asar dimulai ketika bayangan sama dengan panjang benda ditambah bayangan saat matahari di puncak. Al-Mawsili mencatat bahwa hukum untuk fatwa mengikuti posisi para murid.

Fardu (perbuatan wajib) salat dalam mazhab Hanafi dirinci. Mazhab ini membedakan antara fara'id (kewajiban mutlak yang jika ditinggalkan membatalkan salat), wajibat (kewajiban yang jika sengaja ditinggalkan membatalkan namun jika terlupa mengharuskan sujud sahwi), dan sunat (perbuatan yang dianjurkan). Fara'id mencakup: takbiratul ihram (yang harus menggunakan kata-kata spesifik 'Allahu Akbar' menurut mazhab Hanafi — posisi yang lebih ketat dibandingkan mazhab lain), posisi berdiri, membaca al-Fatihah, membaca surah setelah al-Fatihah pada dua rakaat pertama (sebuah wajib yang khas dalam mazhab Hanafi), rukuk, sujud, duduk (qi'dah akhirah), dan salam penutup.

Posisi mazhab Hanafi tentang membaca al-Fatihah sangat penting: ia berstatus wajib bukan fardu. Ini berarti orang yang sama sekali lupa membaca al-Fatihah dan membaca surah Al-Quran lainnya tetap dianggap sah salatnya (meskipun tidak benar), dan ia wajib melakukan sujud sahwi. Ini berbeda tajam dengan mazhab Syafi'i dan Hanbali yang menjadikan al-Fatihah sebagai rukun yang jika ditinggalkan membatalkan salat tanpa pengecualian.

Al-Mawsili membahas posisi mazhab Hanafi tentang salat berjamaah (jama'ah). Mazhab Hanafi menganggap jama'ah sebagai wajib bagi laki-laki dewasa dalam keadaan biasa — bukan sekadar disunahkan (sebagaimana mazhab Maliki dan Syafi'i berpendapat) tetapi tidak sewajib ketat posisi mazhab Hanbali. Meninggalkan jama'ah tanpa uzur yang sah mendatangkan dosa tanpa membatalkan salat itu sendiri.

Peran imam dibahas secara terperinci. Mazhab Hanafi menentukan siapa yang boleh memimpin salat: seorang laki-laki boleh mengimami laki-laki lain, perempuan, atau kelompok campuran. Perempuan tidak boleh mengimami laki-laki. Anak yang telah mencapai usia tamyiz boleh mengimami anak-anak lain tetapi tidak dewasa dalam salat fardu. Salat imam harus setara validitasnya atau lebih baik dari salat makmum; jika salat imam tidak sah karena suatu kondisi yang tidak diketahui makmum, salat makmum tetap sah menurut mazhab Hanafi — sebuah posisi yang sangat penting secara pastoral.

Pembahasan mazhab Hanafi tentang salat witir bersifat khas: ia berstatus wajib (wajib tetapi bukan fardu), dikerjakan tiga rakaat dengan doa qunut pada rakaat ketiga setelah rukuk. Ini berbeda dari witir satu rakaat dalam mazhab Syafi'i dan Maliki, serta witir yang bervariasi rakaatnya dalam mazhab Hanbali.
content_ar: الصلاة in the الحنفي التراث as presented in Al-Ikhtiyar reflects the school's comprehensive approach للعبادة, combining textual evidence with sophisticated legal reasoning. Al-Mawsili begins with the establishment of الصلاة times, grounding each in the الحديث of جبريل ﷺ (the Angel جبريل ﷺ's teaching النبي ﷺ the الصلاة times) and the الحديث of 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr narrated in أبو داود and Al-النسائي. The الحنفي school has some of the most carefully specified الصلاة time rules among the four schools. For 'Asr, the الحنفي school (following الإمام Abu Hanifa) holds that the preferred time begins عندما the shadow of everything is twice its own length plus the meridian shadow — a later starting point than the other three schools. This was الإمام Abu Hanifa's position based on his reading of the relevant hadiths. His two major students, Abu يوسف ﷺ and محمد ﷺ ibn al-حسن, agreed with the other schools that 'Asr begins عندما the shadow equals the object's length plus the meridian shadow. Al-Mawsili notes that the ruling for الفتوى follows the students' position. The farida (obligatory act) of الصلاة in the الحنفي school are enumerated. The school distinguishes between fara'id (absolute obligations whose omission invalidates the الصلاة), wajibat (obligations whose intentional omission invalidates but whose forgetful omission requires السجود as-sahw), and sunan (recommended acts). The fara'id include: the opening التكبير (which must use the specific words 'الله أكبر' وفقاً لـ the الحنفي school — a stricter position than other schools), the standing posture, al-Fatihah, adding a السورة بعد al-Fatihah in the أولاً two rak'ahs (a الواجب unique to the الحنفي school), الركوع', السجود, the sitting position (qi'dah الآخرة), and the closing salam. The الحنفي school's position on reciting al-Fatihah is المهم: it is الواجب rather than الفرض. This means that one who forgets al-Fatihah entirely and recites another القرآني passage instead has still prayed validly (though wrongly), and must perform السجود as-sahw. This differs sharply from the الشافعي and الحنبلي schools, which make al-Fatihah a pillar (rukn) whose omission invalidates the الصلاة regardless. Al-Mawsili discusses the الحنفي position on the congregational الصلاة (jama'ah). The الحنفي school considers jama'ah to be الواجب for adult males in ordinary circumstances — not merely recommended (as the المالكي and الشافعي schools hold) but not as strictly obligatory as the الحنبلي school's position. Missing congregation without valid excuse incurs sinfulness without invalidating the الصلاة itself. The الإمام's role is treated in detail. The الحنفي school specifies who may lead الصلاة: a man may lead other men, women, or mixed groups. A woman may not lead men. A الولد who has reached the age of discernment may lead other الأولاد but not adults in obligatory الصلوات. The الإمام's الصلاة must be at least as valid as that of the followers; إذا the الإمام's الصلاة is invalid بسبب
